header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Catalyst 9300 family, spanning the C9300 (modular uplink), C9300LM, and C9300L (fixed uplink) sub-families. Structure: axis_registry (filterable vocabulary) + catalog (atomic SKU facts) + groups (named shared compatibility sets) + models (switch entries that supply axis values, reference groups, and on fixed-uplink models carry an inline uplink_capacity block in place of a module-group reference).

sources.name: Cisco Catalyst 9300 Series Switches Data Sheet

design_notes: Capability = axis (filterable); the option that realizes it = variable (configured after switch is selected).

Uplink axes are resolved against an uplink_capacity block, never stored per-speed on the switch. On modular models the block lives on the referenced network-module group (look-through); on fixed-uplink models (C9300LM, C9300L) the switch carries an inline uplink_capacity block of the same shape. Both resolve identically.
PoE budget is not a single number: it is a (primary PSU, secondary PSU) -> watts matrix on the switch; the poe_budget_watts axis value is the max across that matrix.
Sharing unit is the SET, not the individual SKU: groups name a shared compatibility set; switches reference the group. Update Cisco's family once -> all referencing switches inherit.
Licensing groups are split on both port-count and tier (a 24-port DNA-E license differs from a 48-port DNA-E license).

License regimes follow the project vocabulary: dna-term, meraki-classic, meraki-subscription, unified (each mapping to a term-based or subscription-based shape). Cisco Catalyst (DNX) is intentionally NOT a regime. All models currently in the file offer dna-term only (tier locked by the -E/-A hardware suffix); the two Meraki regimes belong to the separate -M SKUs; unified does not apply to these entries.
Order-time 'default' selections (CCW cart pre-picks) are intentionally NOT stored: the tool proposes a valid config from requirements rather than mirroring the ordering cart.
Solver is out of scope for this artifact; this file is data shape + axis registry only.

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

network_modules.notes: Datasheet Table 3 footnote: compatible only with C9300X-48HX, C9300X-48TX, and C9300X-24Y. NOT compatible with C9300X-12Y (or other C9300X models).

power_supplies.notes: 1900W HVDC. Listed for C9300 series in HIG Table 1; never observed for sale but present in the install guide. Retained for completeness/filtering.

licenses.notes: Network stack perpetual license, embedded with hardware, included at no separate selection. Tier locked to the switch's -E suffix. Pairs with the term-based subscription above; the regime tag groups it with the DNA program.

Network stack perpetual license, embedded with hardware, included at no separate selection. Tier locked to the switch's -A suffix. Pairs with the term-based subscription above; the regime tag groups it with the DNA program.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Essentials. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Essentials. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Essentials. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Advantage. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Advantage. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300X 25G fiber (12Y/24Y) DNA Advantage. SKU carries no port-count; scoped to the 25G fiber X-class models only.
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-24S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Essentials. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).
C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port DNA Advantage. -S suffix denotes 1G fiber access models (e.g. C9300-48S).

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by name and inherits the whole set. The sharing unit is the set, not the SKU. Defaults intentionally omitted. none_option is the CCW SKU used to remove the category when not required.

uplink_capacity.notes: Aggregated from member provides_uplinks: max provided by any SINGLE member per speed (one module bay -> speeds are mutually exclusive). The uplink_* axes resolve against this block; no per-speed value is stored on the switch. 100G omitted: no C9300-NM member provides it.

network_module_groups.notes: C9300X-NM-* modules are deliberately excluded: they are C9300X-only.

uplink_capacity.notes: Aggregated from member provides_uplinks: max provided by any SINGLE member per speed (one module bay -> speeds mutually exclusive). C9300X network modules (UADP 2.0sec class). 2C/4C recorded as 100G only (single declared speed per module, as 8X is 10G-only and 4G is 1G-only); 40G capability of these QSFP28 modules is left to solver subsumption logic, not stored here.

network_module_groups.notes: C9300X-only network modules (datasheet: C9300X NMs supported only on C9300X models). Full set including C9300X-NM-4C. Use the -12y variant group for the C9300X-12Y, which cannot take the 4C.

uplink_capacity.notes: Same as c9300x-modular-uplink-modules but EXCLUDES C9300X-NM-4C, which the datasheet footnote lists as incompatible with C9300X-12Y. Per-speed maxima drop accordingly (100G from 4 to 2 via the 2C). 2C/4C recorded as 100G only (single declared speed per module, as 8X is 10G-only and 4G is 1G-only); 40G capability of these QSFP28 modules is left to solver subsumption logic, not stored here.

network_module_groups.notes: C9300X-12Y-specific module set: full C9300X NM set minus C9300X-NM-4C (datasheet Table 3 footnote: 4C compatible only with 48HX/48TX/24Y).

power_supply_groups.notes: Membership from Hardware Installation Guide Table 1 (Power Supply Module Part Numbers), pruned to the Platinum (-P) variant wherever a non-Platinum twin exists at the same wattage (non-Platinum units are not proposed/quotable). Which member is valid as PRIMARY and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the switch entry. C9300 (modular uplink) series. Excludes the -UP Platinum-upgrade migration SKUs (PWR-C1-715WAC-UP, PWR-C1-1100WAC-UP), which are not base configurables.

Membership from Hardware Installation Guide Table 1 (Power Supply Module Part Numbers), pruned to the Platinum (-P) variant wherever a non-Platinum twin exists at the same wattage (non-Platinum units are not proposed/quotable). Which member is valid as PRIMARY and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the switch entry. C9300L (fixed uplink) series. Shares the C1 PSU family with C9300/C9300X but the HIG lists a distinct membership; kept as its own named group so it can diverge.

C9300LM-specific PSU set (HIG Table 1 lists C6-600WAC and C6-1KWAC; C6-715WDC retained per project decision for future DC filtering, and is referenced by the LM PoE matrix). C9300LM uses different power supplies than 9300/9300X/9300L. Which member is valid as primary, and the achievable PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair, are switch-specific and live on the switch entry.

Membership from Hardware Installation Guide Table 1 (Power Supply Module Part Numbers), pruned to the Platinum (-P) variant wherever a non-Platinum twin exists at the same wattage (non-Platinum units are not proposed/quotable). Which member is valid as PRIMARY and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the switch entry. C9300X (modular uplink) series. All members already Platinum/DC per HIG.

stack_cable_groups.notes: Fixed-uplink LM/L stacking via C9300L-STACK-KIT2 (two stack adapters + one cable, 50cm default). Type-3A cables. Distinct from the modular Type-1 STACK-T1 set.

C9300L fixed-uplink stacking via C9300L-STACK-KIT2 (two stack adapters + one cable, 50cm default), Type-3A cables. The original C9300L-STACK-KIT/Type-3 (STACK-T3-*) is no longer sold; only KIT2/T3A is proposed. Same stacking hardware as the C9300LM group; kept as its own named group so it can diverge.

C9300X StackWise-1T uses the same Type-1 STACK-T1 cables as C9300 (StackWise-480); membership identical to c9300-modular-stack-cables. Kept as its own named group because the ordering guide uses a distinct none-option (C9300X-STACK-NONE) and a different default cable length, and so the set can diverge.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.notes: C9300X 25G fiber (-L-) DNA Essentials set. SKUs carry no port-count and serve both 12-port and 24-port X-class fiber models; port_class is null rather than a number. Other C9300X models use the C9300 license sets, not this one.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.notes: C9300X 25G fiber (-L-) DNA Advantage set. SKUs carry no port-count and serve both 12-port and 24-port X-class fiber models; port_class is null rather than a number. Other C9300X models use the C9300 license sets, not this one.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.notes: C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port Essentials group. -S suffix licenses are distinct from copper -24 licenses in the ordering guide; kept as a separate group so fiber models reference the correct SKU family.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.notes: C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 24-port Advantage group. -S suffix licenses are distinct from copper -24 licenses in the ordering guide; kept as a separate group so fiber models reference the correct SKU family.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.
Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.notes: C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port Essentials group. -S suffix licenses are distinct from copper -48 licenses in the ordering guide; kept as a separate group so fiber models reference the correct SKU family.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.notes: C9300 1G fiber (SFP) 48-port Advantage group. -S suffix licenses are distinct from copper -48 licenses in the ordering guide; kept as a separate group so fiber models reference the correct SKU family.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.notes: C9300L fixed-uplink 24-port Advantage group. C9300LM models (both 24- and 48-port) also use C9300L license SKUs per Cisco CCW configuration tool; the ordering guide's C9300LM-specific SKUs do not exist in practice.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -E.

license_groups.notes: C9300L fixed-uplink 48-port Essentials group. C9300LM models (both 24- and 48-port) also use C9300L license SKUs per Cisco CCW configuration tool; the ordering guide's C9300LM-specific SKUs do not exist in practice.

term_variable.notes: Term length is a downstream config VARIABLE (not a registry axis): chosen after switch+regime resolve. Subscription is mandatory; tier locked to -A.

license_groups.notes: C9300L fixed-uplink 48-port Advantage group. C9300LM models (both 24- and 48-port) also use C9300L license SKUs per Cisco CCW configuration tool; the ordering guide's C9300LM-specific SKUs do not exist in practice.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9300, 48-port (36x 100M/1G/2.5G + 12x Multigigabit 10G/5G/2.5G/1G/100M) copper, Cisco UPOE, modular uplinks, Network Essentials. ENERGY STAR certified.

axis_values._comment: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. These are what the solver filters/ranks on.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How look-through axis values were derived, so they can be re-validated.
axis_value_provenance.uplink_*: Not stored on the model. Resolved against c9300-modular-uplink-modules.uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (uplink_modular=true).

attributes._comment: Displayed, never filtered. Kept for reference; not axes.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + the switch-specific overlay only where facts vary per model.

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet Table 4, C9300-48UXM rows (1100W default / 1900W upgrade).

license.tier_lock_reason: -E suffix forces Network Essentials perpetual and an Essentials-tier subscription (subscription tier must match network stack tier).

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.

power_cables.note: Region-specific cable must be chosen at order time; not a selection axis.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9300 mini (LM), 48-port (8x Multigigabit 10G/5G/2.5G/1G/100M + 40x 1G) copper, Cisco UPOE, 4x 25G SFP28 fixed uplinks, Network Advantage.

axis_values._comment: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. These are what the solver filters/ranks on.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How look-through / resolved axis values were derived, so they can be re-validated.
axis_value_provenance.poe_budget_watts: max of poe_budget_matrix below (1790). Datasheet C9300LM PoE table, C9300LM-48UX-4Y row.
axis_value_provenance.stackpower_capable: false: datasheet states StackPower is supported only on modular uplink models (C9300/C9300X SKUs); Table 1 shows StackPower X for 9300LM.
axis_value_provenance.stacking_technology: stackwise-320: datasheet Table 5, C9300LM SKUs via C9300L-STACK-KIT2.
axis_value_provenance.uplink_*: Not from a module group (uplink_modular=false). Resolved against inline configurables.uplinks.uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (fixed 4x 25G).

attributes._comment: Displayed, never filtered. Kept for reference; not axes.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + the switch-specific overlay. Uplinks are inline because uplink_modular=false (fixed SKU uplinks).

uplink_capacity.notes: Fixed 4x 25G SFP28 uplinks soldered into the SKU. No module group, no bays: the inline per_speed_max IS the resolved capacity the uplink_* axes check against. uplink_25g >= N resolves flat here, identically to the modular case.

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet C9300LM PoE supply table, C9300LM-48UX-4Y row (PWR-C6-1KWAC default primary; secondary columns 600W AC / 715W DC / 1000W AC).

license.tier_lock_reason: -A suffix forces Network Advantage perpetual and an Advantage-tier subscription (subscription tier must match network stack tier). C9300LM uses standard C9300L license SKUs (C9300L-DNA-A-48-*) per Cisco CCW; no C9300LM-specific license SKUs exist in practice.

ssd_accessory.notes: Required accessory kit to install an SSD on a C9300LM (ordering guide). Option/variable, not an axis; included here for completeness, carries no filterable axis.

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.

power_cables.note: Region-specific cable must be chosen at order time; not a selection axis.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9300L, 24-port 1G data (no PoE) copper, 4x 1G SFP fixed uplinks, Network Essentials. Fixed-uplink C9300L (StackWise-320, no StackPower).

axis_values._comment: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. These are what the solver filters/ranks on.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How resolved axis values were derived, so they can be re-validated.
axis_value_provenance.poe_*: poe_capable=false: datasheet Table 2 C9300L-24T-4G row shows 'No PoE' across all PSU columns (data model, 350W default). poe_type='none'; poe_budget_watts / poe_port_count omitted (optional axes, absent when not poe_capable).
axis_value_provenance.stackpower_capable: false: datasheet states StackPower is supported only on modular uplink models (C9300/C9300X SKUs); Table 1 shows StackPower X for C9300L.
axis_value_provenance.stacking_technology: stackwise-320: datasheet Table 5, C9300L SKUs, via C9300L-STACK-KIT2.
axis_value_provenance.uplink_*: Not from a module group (uplink_modular=false). Resolved against inline configurables.uplinks.uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (fixed 4x 1G).

attributes._comment: Displayed, never filtered. Kept for reference; not axes.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + switch-specific overlay. Uplinks inline because uplink_modular=false (fixed SKU uplinks). No PoE matrix: this is a no-PoE model.

uplink_capacity.notes: Fixed 4x 1G SFP uplinks in the SKU (-4G suffix). No module group, no bays: the inline per_speed_max IS the resolved capacity the uplink_* axes check against. uplink_1g >= N resolves flat here, identically to the modular case.

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet Table 2 + PSU table: C9300L-24T-4G default PWR-C1-350WAC-P, 'No PoE' across all secondary columns. Empty matrix => no PoE budget (poe_capable=false). Secondary PSU still orderable for power redundancy; redundant_psu_capable=true.

license.tier_lock_reason: -E suffix forces Network Essentials perpetual and an Essentials-tier subscription (subscription tier must match network stack tier).

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.

power_cables.note: Ships with one PSU by default (second is optional for redundancy). Region-specific cable chosen at order time; not a selection axis.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9300X, 12-port 25G/10G/1G SFP28 fiber, modular uplinks, no PoE, Network Essentials.

axis_values._comment: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. These are what the solver filters/ranks on.

axis_value_provenance._comment: How resolved axis values were derived, so they can be re-validated.
axis_value_provenance.poe_*: poe_capable=false: datasheet Table 4 C9300X-12Y row shows 'No PoE' across all secondary-PSU columns (PWR-C1-715WAC-P default). poe_type='none'; poe_budget_watts/poe_port_count omitted (optional axes, absent when not poe_capable).
axis_value_provenance.multigig_port_count: 0: the 12 access ports are 25G/10G/1G SFP28 fiber (Table 2), not copper Multigigabit (mGig = copper >1G over RJ-45). Fiber SFP28 speed is not counted as mGig.
axis_value_provenance.stacking_technology: stackwise-1t: datasheet Table 1/Table 5, C9300X SKUs (1 Tbps; 480G when stacked with a C9300).
axis_value_provenance.stackpower_capable: true: datasheet states StackPower supported on C9300 and C9300X SKUs; Table 1 shows StackPower checked for C9300X (larger budget / StackPower+).
axis_value_provenance.uplink_*: Not stored on the model (uplink_modular=true). Resolved against c9300x-12y-uplink-modules.uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (the 12Y cannot take C9300X-NM-4C).
axis_value_provenance.access_*_fiber: 12x SFP28 access ports, each 1/10/25G capable (datasheet Table 2, C9300X-12Y row: 25G/10G/1G SFP28). All 12 ports support all three speeds, so each fiber access axis = 12. Capability declared in data (not derived by the solver from 25G).

attributes._comment: Displayed, never filtered. Kept for reference; not axes.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + switch-specific overlay. No PoE matrix: this is a no-PoE model. Uplinks modular -> reference the 12Y module group (4C excluded).

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet Table 4: C9300X-12Y default PWR-C1-715WAC-P, 'No PoE' across all secondary columns. Empty matrix => no PoE budget (poe_capable=false). Secondary PSU still orderable for power redundancy; redundant_psu_capable=true.

license.tier_lock_reason: -E suffix forces Network Essentials perpetual and an Essentials-tier subscription (subscription tier must match network stack tier).

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.

power_cables.note: Region-specific cable must be chosen at order time; not a selection axis.

models.description: Cisco Catalyst 9300X, 12-port 25G/10G/1G SFP28 fiber, modular uplinks, no PoE, Network Advantage.

axis_values._comment: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. These are what the solver filters/ranks on.

axis_value_provenance.poe_*: poe_capable=false: datasheet Table 4 C9300X-12Y row shows 'No PoE' across all secondary-PSU columns (PWR-C1-715WAC-P default). poe_type='none'; poe_budget_watts/poe_port_count omitted (optional axes, absent when not poe_capable).
axis_value_provenance.multigig_port_count: 0: the 12 access ports are 25G/10G/1G SFP28 fiber (Table 2), not copper Multigigabit (mGig = copper >1G over RJ-45). Fiber SFP28 speed is not counted as mGig.
axis_value_provenance.stacking_technology: stackwise-1t: datasheet Table 1/Table 5, C9300X SKUs (1 Tbps; 480G when stacked with a C9300).
axis_value_provenance.stackpower_capable: true: datasheet states StackPower supported on C9300 and C9300X SKUs; Table 1 shows StackPower checked for C9300X (larger budget / StackPower+).
axis_value_provenance.uplink_*: Not stored on the model (uplink_modular=true). Resolved against c9300x-12y-uplink-modules.uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (the 12Y cannot take C9300X-NM-4C).
axis_value_provenance.access_*_fiber: 12x SFP28 access ports, each 1/10/25G capable (datasheet Table 2, C9300X-12Y row: 25G/10G/1G SFP28). All 12 ports support all three speeds, so each fiber access axis = 12. Capability declared in data (not derived by the solver from 25G).

attributes._comment: Displayed, never filtered. Kept for reference; not axes.

configurables._comment: Thin references to shared groups + switch-specific overlay. No PoE matrix: this is a no-PoE model. Uplinks modular -> reference the 12Y module group (4C excluded).

power_supplies.matrix_provenance: Datasheet Table 4: C9300X-12Y default PWR-C1-715WAC-P, 'No PoE' across all secondary columns. Empty matrix => no PoE budget (poe_capable=false). Secondary PSU still orderable for power redundancy; redundant_psu_capable=true.

license.tier_lock_reason: -A suffix forces Network Advantage perpetual and an Advantage-tier subscription (subscription tier must match network stack tier).

included_by_default_non_selectable._comment: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.
